docs.summary:
Generate a link to the user's Editor, and open app market to allow the user to add the app to the stage
 If this API is called by the browser in a full redirect (accept: text/html) then it will redirect to the resulted link
 Otherwise, it will return EditorDeepLinkResponse

members.doc: The schema of the payload that will be passed with trigger when activating an Automation.

members.name: actionRecommendations
members.doc: List of suggestions passed to actions when setting up an automation, e.g. A suggested message for the Send Chat Message action.

members.doc: Info for setting up the trigger in reengagement mode, e.g. "Client hasn't checked-in in X days".

members.name: fields
members.doc: the fields of the payload

members.name: dynamicSchemaUrl
members.doc:
the endpoint to resolve the schema configuration after the user updates the option selections.
invocation will be done via REST protocol, method POST with payload as described in com.wixpress.actiontriggers.integrator.entities.DynamicSchemaRequest
expected output must be compliant with com.wixpress.actiontriggers.integrator.entities.DynamicSchemaResponse
in order to insure the caller identity we will add a HEADER, 'md5', that will be signed with a private key and can be decrypted using the RSA 1024 public key,
MIGfMA0GCSqGSIb3DQEBAQUAA4GNADCBiQKBgQCuwhNPTeyleWLcq7nVGyXv1Swar442JNZ7fKSukPjuoNUokw1X+fvQ7GcqQu5SOqCRhlBjnL0HAjUVeUvBAHotmGXbzBpe73uKsj6am5XiRubxFT3t17teAR4KYvn1d7izaxGkc+eyAZm7M/9tvrms5DGMvilZ01zusWek1i3FmwIDAQAB

the decrypted md5 header must match md5 of the body.

example call:
curl --location --request POST 'www.trigger-example.com/dynamic-schema' \
--header 'md5: Lu9Gk1EavSV23vKno5pNuKBNDFn0H5il2hsfl0nsKsF186EpJFNVrMEVGCM/CYF9YYIOu0PzprCJBT9i7dKVv+6Scp9FhRz3kiKWOTfMEbFW+gIwOeQ3OojQ6VVA9668CDKe+rsWmAXVDPXASXtdf2849BNJ6SZkC2vsBF9yRbE=' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--data-raw '{"selectedConditions":{"someKey":{"values":["value1","value2"]},"anotherKey":{"values":["anotherValue1","anotherValue2"]}},"instanceId":"2ad0b21b-2813-4380-b56c-a8ded8aa854f"}'

resolving side validation:
1. decrypt the md5 header using the public key specified above -> in the example above the result should be -> A159933841647D2A1579001CB49C0B42
2. run md5 on the payload -> 65DAEF72E2A8BC42C8A9CC2042406C7C

example result:
{
"schemaConfiguration": {
"fields": [
{
"key": "exampleKey",
"displayName": "Example Key",
"description": "Some example",
"fieldType": "TEXT",
"sampleValues": [
"Hello World"
],
"exposure": "EXPOSED",
"allowNegativeOffset": false
}
]
}
}

docs.summary: need to think about it

members.name: allowNegativeOffset
members.doc: default: false, set this to true if the date is normally a future date, like: Invoice expiration date, Membership renewal date

docs.summary: Set of key-value pairs.enumValue (The field value option) -> enum option

members.name: key:value
members.doc: `key` of type string, `value` of type string

members.name: options
members.doc: Set of key-value pairs.enumValue (The field value option) -> enum option

members.name: date
members.doc:
the field value must conform to the following JSON structure { "timestamp": string, "timeZone": string }, where the timestamp is ISO format UTC, and the time zone is the full name
 example: { "timestamp": "2021-03-22T11:41:47.992Z", timeZone: "Asia/Jerusalem" }

members.name: key
members.doc: The key as it appears on the payload.

members.name: displayName
members.doc: The name displayed to the user.

members.name: fieldType
members.doc: the field type

members.name: sampleValues
members.doc: sample values, values that could show up in the payload of a trigger.

members.name: exposure
members.doc: the field exposure

members.name: conditionOptions
members.doc: for fields with a known set of possible values

members.name: displayName
members.doc: The name displayed to the user, e.g. "Select a Product:"

members.name: values

Namespace display name.
 Your labels will be grouped under this display name in the Contacts UI
 (e.g. Email Marketing)

members.doc: Allow app-defined labels (Check this box to allow apps to create labels in this namespace.)

members.doc:
Required permissions to manage app-defined labels
 Only apps with this permission will be able to create labels in this namespace.
 relevant if allow_app_defined_labels is true
 (e.g. EMAIL_MARKETING.MANAGE_LABELS)

Predefined labels
 These labels exist on all sites that install the app.

Label key
 Can contain letters, dashes, or underscores.
 (e.g. my-label)

members.name: displayName

Display Name
 Human-readable name to be shown on the UI.
 (e.g. My Label)

members.name: widgetComponentData
members.doc:
Create a widget iframe that users can display on their site. We recommend submitting a proposal before using iframes in your app.
 Learn More: https://devforum.wix.com/en/article/widget-components

members.name: pageComponentData
members.doc:
Display an iframe as a page on a user's site. Submit a proposal to use it.
 Learn More: https://devforum.wix.com/en/article/page-components

members.name: dashboardComponentData
members.doc:
Open an iframe in the Dashboard, or add a link to a page on your platform.
 Learn More: https://devforum.wix.com/en/article/create-a-dashboard-page-component

members.name: workerComponentData
members.doc:
Use an invisible iframe to track activity. It’s added to every page on the site (max. 1).
 Learn More: https://devforum.wix.com/en/article/worker-components

members.name: studioComponentData
members.doc:
Use Wix Blocks to create a widget component. Wix Blocks will open in a new tab.
 Learn More: https://bo.wix.com/wix-docs/rest/drafts/wix-blocks-server---app-builder

members.name: embeddedScriptComponentData
members.doc:
Inject third party scripts into the user’s site.
 Learn More: https://devforum.wix.com/en/article/about-embedded-script-components

members.name: version
members.doc:
The calculated version from the request
 (1) for specific version (e.g 1.2.3) - will return the specific version (e.g 1.2.3)
 (2) for semantic version (e.g ^1.2.3) - will return the semantic version (e.g 1.2.3)
 (3) for "latest" version - will return the latest version of the app (common in 'test your app' flow)
 (4) for None version - will return the last version we will find for this app. first approved version and then the published, draft or hidden version.

members.doc: optional, will forwarded to app-service to check if the requesting user is allowed to installed this app when the user isn't an owner OR this version isn't published

members.name: version
members.doc: version of the app that was eventually installed

members.name: instanceId
members.doc: will return only on responses, cannot be changed

members.name: requiredActions
members.doc: additional steps required to finish the installation of the app on the site

docs.summary:
Required steps could be the following:
OAUTH2 -> In thise case no link will return, the client will need to run finishInstallation method (described here) with parameters
EMBEDDED_SCRIPT -> No link will return, the client will need to use either api or the auto generated form to embed the parameters
EDITOR -> A link (with deeplink) to add the app to the site will be provided to the user's editor
DASHBOARD -> A link will be provided to either the user's BM inside the app (for an embedded dashboard) or to the developers dashboard url (for a none embeded dashboard)

docs.summary:
Possible values are:
  • NONE_EXTENSION
  • PAYMENTS_GATEWAY_EXTENSION
  • COUPONS_EXTENSION
  • DROPSHIPPING_EXTENSION
  • FULFILMENT_EXTENSION
  • DROPSHIPPING_SUPPLIER_EXTENSION
  • FULFILLMENT_CENTER_EXTENSION
  • RESTAURANTS_POS_EXTENSION
  • ART_STORE_EXTENSION
  • ASCEND_AUTOMATION_EXTENSION
  • CONTACT_LABELS_EXTENSION

Category of pages this will be embedded on (single, many, none) Possible values are:
  • NONE_PAGES - It will not be embedded
  • ONCE - It will be embedded once
  • SPECIFIC_PAGES - It will be embedded multiple times on specific pages

Where that embed will be rendered Possible values are:
  • NONE_PLACEMENT - It will not be rendered
  • HEAD - In the document head
  • BODY_START - Prepended before all children already rendered in the body tag
  • BODY_END - Appended after the last child already rendered in the body tag

Embed category defined for Privacy regulation compliance in EU and CCPA in the US Possible values are:
  • UNKNOWN - Not categorized yet
  • ESSENTIAL - Must load regardless of policy
  • FUNCTIONAL - Adds optional functionality to the site
  • ANALYTICS - Adds analytics abilities to the site
  • ADVERTISING - Adds advertising content or advertising tracking to the site

Possible values are:
  • NO_UNIT
  • AUTO
  • PX
  • VH
  • VW
  • PERCENTAGE

Possible values are:
  • NO_HDOCKING
  • LEFT_DOCKING
  • HCENTER
  • RIGHT_DOCKING

Possible values are:
  • NO_VDOCKING
  • TOP_DOCKING
  • VCENTER
  • BOTTOM_DOCKING

Bar alignment selected value Possible values are:
  • ALIGN_LEFT
  • ALIGN_CENTER
  • ALIGN_RIGHT

Possible values are:
  • SMALL
  • MEDIUM
  • LARGE
  • XLARGE

Possible values are:
  • WIDGET
  • PAGE

Vertical widget position in the browser window Possible values are:
  • NONE_VERTICAL
  • TOP
  • CENTER_VERTICAL
  • BOTTOM

Horizontal widget position in the browser window Possible values are:
  • NONE_HORIZONTAL
  • LEFT
  • CENTER_HORIZONTAL
  • RIGHT

Widget will automatically be stretched to this width after installing it Possible values are:
  • NONE_TYPE
  • CUSTOM - A custom width for the widget
  • FULL - Full width of the browser window

Possible values are:
  • no_region
  • header
  • pageContainer
  • footer

Possible values are:
  • UNKNOWN_SIMPLE_TYPE
  • MONEY
  • LINK
  • BACKOFFICE_LINK
  • LIVESITE_LINK
  • MULTILINGUAL
  • IMAGE_LINK
  • GUID

Possible values are:
  • UNKNOWN_PRIMITIVE_TYPE
  • TEXT
  • BOOLEAN
  • NUMBER

Possible values are:
  • UNKNOWN_EVENT_TYPE
  • LEGACY
  • DOMAIN_EVENT

Possible values are:
  • UNKNOWN_EXPOSURE
  • SETUP - not sent to the action provider
  • HIDDEN - sent to the action provider and usually hidden from user
  • EXPOSED - sent to the action provider and usually shown to user

Possible values are:
  • UNKNOWN_SELECTION_TYPE
  • SINGLE
  • MULTI

Possible values are:
  • UNKNOWN_VISIBILITY
  • VISIBLE
  • READONLY
  • HIDDEN

Possible values are:
  • UNKNOWN_ACTION_VISIBILITY_OVERRIDE
  • HIDE
  • SHOW

Possible values are:
  • UNKNOWN_REENGAGEMENT_MODE
  • DISABLED
  • ENABLED

Possible values are:
  • UNKNOWN_PRECONDITION_TYPE
  • PETRI
  • STATE
  • APP_DEPENDENCY
  • DEALER_OFFERING

Possible Partial Payment Policy Values Possible values are:
  • UNDEFINED
  • ALLOW - Allow Partial Payment
  • DISALLOW - Disallow Partial Payment

Component type Possible values are:
  • NONE
  • STUDIO
  • PLATFORM
  • WORKER
  • DASHBOARD
  • WIDGET
  • PAGE
  • DASHBOARD_PLATFORM
  • STUDIO_WIDGET
  • EMBEDDED_SCRIPT
  • EXTENSION
  • SNIPPET_SOLUTION
  • DATA_COMPONENT
  • WEB
  • DC_CONFIG
  • WIDGET_OUT_OF_IFRAME
  • PAGE_OUT_OF_IFRAME
  • STATIC_FILE
  • APP_CONFIG
  • MULTIPLE_DASHBOARDS
  • PAYMENTS_GATEWAY
  • CODE_PACKAGE
  • AUTOMATION_TRIGGER
  • INVOICES_ACTIONS
  • DASHBOARD_APPLICATION
  • CONTACT_LABELS
  • BILLY_TEST

Possible values are:
  • VIDEO
  • IMAGE
  • YOUTUBE

Possible values are:
  • NONE_VALUE
  • NEW_WINDOW
  • NEW_TAB

Possible values are:
  • NONE_ACTION
  • NO_ACTION
  • REMOVE_ALL_PRODUCT
  • SHIPPING_RATES_REGION

Possible values are:
  • UNKNOWN_DESCRIPTION
  • BENEFITS - indicate that the description will be list of benefits
  • CUSTOM_TEXT - indicate that the description will custom_text field

Possible values are:
  • UNKNOWN
  • VISIBLE - show this plan on app market
  • HIDDEN - hide this plan

Possible values are:
  • None
  • FREEMIUM
  • PREMIUM
  • FREE
  • WIX_PREMIUM
  • DYNAMIC

Possible values are:
  • NONE_STATUS - default, internal use status. do not use
  • DRAFT - a version which was created in the dev center and has not started the approval process yet
  • SUBMITTED - the version was submitted for review by the developer
  • IN_REVIEW - the version is being reviewed by the app market team in cactus
  • APPROVED - our team approved the version
  • PUBLISHED - this version is published - only one version can be published at any given time; if this app was already published, the previously published version's status is now ARCHIVED
  • DECLINED - our team did not accept this version of the app
  • ARCHIVED - the status for versions that are no longer relevant
  • HIDDEN - status for versions with no significant changes, e.g. for test app. for questions contact our team
  • RELEASED - mark apps as RELEASED, without adding to the apps cache, and allow users to install

Possible values are:
  • NO_ROLE - Not supported
  • OWNER - View, edit
  • DEVELOPER - Not supported
  • ADMIN - View, edit
  • GOD - App market teams role. Do not use.

Possible values are:
  • UNKNOWN
  • AUTO_RENEW_ON - Subscription renewed when current period ends with automatic payment method, for example credit card
  • AUTO_RENEW_OFF - Subscription is turned off at current period end date
  • MANUAL - Subscription is renewed when current period ends only if paid for manually

Possible values are:
  • EMPTY
  • INSTALLED - the app was installed on this meta site id
  • PENDING - meaning the app needs the user to finish the installation (for example add the app in the editor)

Possible values are:
  • NONE
  • OAUTH2
  • EMBEDDED_SCRIPT
  • EDITOR
  • DASHBOARD
